methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
SunDrive Solar is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised A$60M+ including a A$25.3M ARENA grant as of November 2025. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA) across the climate and energy sector, with the most relevant peers being series-A stage companies including Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base of $4M and applying a typical 2.5x seed-to-next-stage multiplier, implies a $10M valuation. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector comparables at series-A stage (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, Steady Energy at $110M) yields a median implied valuation of $150M, with a range of $110M to $200M. The peer-median approach carries greater weight given SunDrive's capital raise and sector traction relative to early-stage funding benchmarks.

Triangulating both methods produces a low estimate of $8.5M, a median of $80M, and a high of $172.5M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around whether SunDrive's current trajectory aligns with series-A peer valuations or earlier-stage multiples. Key sensitivities include near-term commercialization progress, capital efficiency relative to peers, and the quality of IP supporting the company's technology differentiation.
